evidence.q1: Section numbering runs 1 through 15 sequentially; cross-reference 'Notwithstanding Section 5' in Section 7 resolves to Section 5.
evidence.q2: 'Customer Assets' is defined in Section 2 and used consistently thereafter in Sections 3,4,5,6,7,8,10,11,12,13,14.
evidence.q3: Section 7: 'Custodian will process release requests in the ordinary course when staffing, chain conditions, and portfolio review procedures permit' contains vague temporal phrasing ('in the ordinary course').
evidence.q4: No archaic legal diction (e.g., heretofore, witnesseth, aforesaid) appears; language such as 'entered into by and between' is standard modern register.
evidence.q5: All sentences are structurally complete and lack internal contradiction; no grammatically broken constructions were found.
evidence.q6: Register is consistent throughout; all sections employ similar modern contractual phrasing (e.g., 'will' to express obligations) without authorial shifts.
